title: Breakfast Relay
desc: The morning meal has to reach the upper gallery without ever traveling in a straight line.

steps.text:
At ordinary inns, breakfast travels from kitchen to dining room along a hallway and perhaps a staircase. At Castle Bodhran, breakfast leaves the kitchen on a tray, disappears around a corner, rises along a wall, crosses a gallery overhead, and emerges in the upper hall as if this had been the obvious route all along.

This morning the breakfast steward is short one server and visibly unwilling to trust hot tea to confused travelers. A tray of flatbread, fruit, and a bronze kettle waits at the kitchen rail. Beside it hangs the dumbwaiter basket used for heavier loads. "The tray route is faster if your hands are steady," the steward says. "The basket route is safer if your nerves are not. Both end in the upper gallery, assuming you don't feed the curtains by accident."

She hands you either the tray cloth or the lift rope. "Carry the breakfast service by hand, or work the dumbwaiter and send the heavy dishes up first."

options.label: Carry the breakfast tray along the handrail route
Operate the dumbwaiter and lift the dishes

steps.text:
The tray route is built around a continuous brass handrail that guides staff through the castle's less reasonable decisions. You balance the tray with one hand and keep the other on the rail as the passage climbs a wall, levels into a corridor, and turns into an overhead bridge above the entry hall. The tea in the kettle wavers dangerously but never spills.

Halfway through, a guest opens the wrong door below you and looks up to find breakfast apparently crossing the ceiling. They stare. You nod as professionally as possible and keep moving. By the time you reach the upper gallery, the tray still looks composed and the tea still deserves to be called tea.

The breakfast steward meets you there with clear relief. "Excellent," she says, taking the kettle. "Now the guests may begin their day by being impressed with the castle instead of by wearing porridge." Around Castle Bodhran, that counts as successful hospitality.

options.label: Set the plates and call the service complete

steps.text:
The dumbwaiter basket hangs in a vertical shaft that is only vertical from one side. From the landing above, it arrives horizontally through a service hatch in the wall. You load the heavier dishes first, balance the basket with counterweights, and pull the rope hand over hand while listening for the bell signal from the upper gallery.

The system works beautifully when the weight is even and embarrassingly when it is not. On the first lift, one platter slides just enough to knock against the basket rim. You lower it, reset the cloth padding, and try again. This time the basket rises smooth and steady, disappearing into the shaft with only the rattle of pulleys and the smell of warm bread following it.

A bell rings once above - received. The next load follows faster: fruit bowl, cups, tea chest, finally the cutlery tray. By the last pull, the breakfast steward leans over the upper hatch and gives you a satisfied wave. "Perfect. We remain a functioning castle for another morning."

options.label: Tie off the rope and send up the final cups
